gate_mapping.G1: T0 green and calibrated cost-model numbers only
gate_mapping.G2: T3 pipeline correctness plus MoE logit parity
gate_mapping.G3: T4 heterogeneous serve at predicted throughput plus correctness
gate_mapping.G4: T4 elasticity with zero dropped in-flight requests
gate_mapping.G5: productized benchmark methodology and operator handoff

reproducibility_controls: version manifest includes code, model, runtime, driver, and hardware
environment and thermal posture recorded for lab-reference
raw logs retained with ledger record

glossary.md.markdown:
# Fornax Glossary

Defines plan IDs, logical hosts, placement plans, T1 simulation, benchmark of record, lab-reference, drain, rollback, node replacement, and gate terminology.
glossary.md.title: Fornax Glossary

onboarding/benchmark-methodology.md.markdown:
# Benchmark Methodology Of Record

The benchmark of record runs on `lab-reference` with commands, prompts or traces, versions, correctness artifacts, environment, raw logs, and a ledger record. Simulation evidence cannot replace T2-T4 lab evidence.
onboarding/benchmark-methodology.md.title: Benchmark Methodology Of Record

onboarding/developer-workflow.md.markdown:
# Fornax Developer Workflow

Run `make fornax-golden`, `make fornax-test`, and a T1 bundle before committing milestone work. Record review-lens findings in `fornax_development_journal.md`.
onboarding/developer-workflow.md.title: Fornax Developer Workflow

onboarding/operator-runbook.md.markdown:
# Fornax Operator Runbook

Operator actions must drain before mutation, verify health after each mutation, preserve plan-integrity tags, and keep dropped in-flight request counts at zero.
onboarding/operator-runbook.md.title: Fornax Operator Runbook

onboarding/quickstart.md.markdown:
# Fornax Operator Quickstart

Use the two-logical-host simulation before real cluster work. Run `python3 -m fornax program simulate-t1`, then inspect the validation bundle and warnings before moving to lab hardware.
onboarding/quickstart.md.title: Fornax Operator Quickstart

glossary_terms.definition: Stable identifier propagated through placement, transport, serving, and evidence artifacts.
A simulated host used to exercise multi-host semantics on local hardware without claiming real cluster evidence.
The planner output that maps layers, stages, replicas, and explanations to nodes.
CI-safe simulated-worker evidence used for development before T2-T4 lab validation.
The reproducible lab-reference benchmark used for gate-grade performance claims.
Controlled heterogeneous lab target for benchmark-of-record runs and T2-T4 evidence.
Stop admitting new work to a node and let in-flight work complete before mutation.
Return a node or deployment to the previous known-good version after a failed or rejected change.
Remove a drained node, admit a replacement, verify health, and restore traffic.
Sponsor decision point with PROCEED, ITERATE, NARROW, or KILL outcomes.

note: T1 onboarding methodology simulation: validates I3 documentation, glossary, and benchmark-methodology structure. Not G5 product-GA closure evidence.

checklist: run doctor and T1 simulation before lab deployment
drain before upgrade, restart, rollback, or node replacement
attach benchmark-of-record evidence before performance claims
escalate when evidence tier does not match gate claim

escalation: fixture cannot be regenerated from CLI

first_run_commands: python3 -m fornax benchmark --plan placement.json --mode tiny-moe-or-expert-mlp --out benchmark.json
success_evidence: correctness evidence attached before throughput claim

escalation: DoD cannot be mapped to a validator
success_evidence: glossary covers gate and benchmark vocabulary
